 {"id":"bd-leo9","title":"Add DCG to CI/CD workflow matrix","description":"## Task: Add DCG to CI/CD workflow matrix

### What to Do

Update GitHub Actions workflows to include DCG in the test matrix and add DCG-specific validation steps.

### Files to Modify

1. `.github/workflows/installer.yml` - Add DCG to installer test matrix
2. `.github/workflows/web.yml` - Add DCG page tests
3. `.github/workflows/checksum-monitor.yml` - Add DCG to trusted tools

### Changes for installer.yml

```yaml
# Add DCG to the test matrix
jobs:
  test-installer:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        component:
          - base
          - claude
          - uv
          - dcg  # ADD THIS
          - caam
          # ... rest of components

  # Add DCG-specific job
  test-dcg:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install DCG
        run: |
          curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/dangerous_command_guard/main/install.sh | bash
          source ~/.bashrc

      - name: Verify DCG installation
        run: |
          command -v dcg
          dcg --version

      - name: Run DCG smoke test
        run: ./tests/vm/dcg_smoke_test.sh

      - name: Run DCG functional test
        run: ./tests/vm/dcg_functional_test.sh --verbose

      - name: Verify DCG blocks dangerous commands
        run: |
          # Test that DCG correctly identifies dangerous command
          dcg test 'git reset --hard' 2>&1 | grep -qi "deny\|block"

      - name: Verify DCG allows safe commands
        run: |
          # Test that DCG allows safe commands
          dcg test 'git status' 2>&1 | grep -qi "allow"
```

### Changes for web.yml

```yaml
jobs:
  test-web:
    steps:
      # ... existing steps ...

      - name: Run Playwright DCG tests
        run: npx playwright test dcg_pages.spec.ts

      - name: Upload DCG test screenshots
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dcg-test-screenshots
          path: test-results/
```

### Changes for checksum-monitor.yml

```yaml
# Add DCG to trusted_tools in the checksum verification
env:
  TRUSTED_TOOLS: |
    claude
    uv
    dcg
```

### Why This Matters

CI/CD integration ensures:
1. DCG installation doesn't break with new releases
2. DCG blocking behavior is continuously verified
3. Website DCG pages render correctly
4. Checksum changes are detected and reviewed

### Acceptance Criteria

- DCG tests run on every PR
- DCG failures block merge
- Test artifacts uploaded on failure
- Checksum changes trigger review workflow","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:04:18.559815578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:51:37.028007173Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:51:37.028007173Z","close_reason":"Completed: DCG tests created and CI updated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-leo9","depends_on_id":"bd-auku","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:06:34.765500093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-leo9","depends_on_id":"bd-vlz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:06:15.283085427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-leo9","author":"ubuntu","text":"FIX NEEDED: Change 'dangerous_command_guard' to 'destructive_command_guard' in curl URL","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:30:20Z"},{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-leo9","author":"ubuntu","text":"ALSO FIX: Branch should be 'master' not 'main' in the curl URL","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:44:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lhe","title":"[ubu.10] Add user communication and progress tracking","description":"# Add User Communication and Progress Tracking

## Purpose
Provide visibility to users during the multi-reboot upgrade process, especially when they SSH back in.

## Problem
When a user SSH reconnects during/after an upgrade:
1. They have no idea what state the system is in
2. They might run install.sh again (could conflict)
3. They might try to use apt (could fail during upgrade)

## Solutions

### 1. MOTD (Message of the Day) Update

Create temporary MOTD during upgrade:
```bash
ubuntu_set_upgrade_motd() {
  cat > /etc/motd.d/99-acfs-upgrade <<EOF
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  ACFS Ubuntu Upgrade in Progress
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Original version: $ORIGINAL_VERSION
  Target version:   $TARGET_VERSION
  Current step:     $CURRENT_STEP
  
  Progress: $COMPLETED_HOPS / $TOTAL_HOPS upgrades complete
  
  To check status:  cat /var/lib/acfs/upgrade_state.json | jq .
  To view logs:     tail -f /var/log/acfs/upgrade.log
  
  DO NOT run apt or install.sh while upgrade is in progress.
  
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
EOF
}

ubuntu_clear_upgrade_motd() {
  rm -f /etc/motd.d/99-acfs-upgrade
}
```

### 2. Lock File to Prevent Concurrent Runs

Prevent install.sh from running during upgrade:
```bash
ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK="/var/run/acfs-upgrade.lock"

ubuntu_acquire_lock() {
  if [[ -f "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK" ]]; then
    local pid=$(cat "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK")
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      log_error "Another upgrade is in progress (PID: $pid)"
      return 1
    fi
    # Stale lock file
    rm -f "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK"
  fi
  echo $$ > "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK"
}

ubuntu_release_lock() {
  rm -f "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK"
}
```

### 3. Status Check Command

Create simple status script:
```bash
# /var/lib/acfs/check_upgrade_status.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
if [[ -f /var/lib/acfs/upgrade_state.json ]]; then
  echo "ACFS Ubuntu Upgrade Status"
  echo "=========================="
  cat /var/lib/acfs/upgrade_state.json | jq -r "
    \"Original: \" + .ubuntu_upgrade.original_version,
    \"Target: \" + .ubuntu_upgrade.target_version,
    \"Current: \" + ((.ubuntu_upgrade.completed_upgrades | length | tostring) + \"/\" + (.ubuntu_upgrade.upgrade_path | length | tostring)),
    \"Stage: \" + .ubuntu_upgrade.current_stage
  "
else
  echo "No upgrade in progress"
fi
```

### 4. Pre-Reboot Warning

Give users time to read before reboot:
```bash
ubuntu_warn_reboot() {
  log_warn "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
  log_warn "║  System will reboot in 30 seconds for Ubuntu upgrade     ║"
  log_warn "║                                                          ║"
  log_warn "║  Your SSH session will disconnect.                       ║"
  log_warn "║  Wait 2-3 minutes, then reconnect.                       ║"
  log_warn "║  The upgrade will continue automatically.                ║"
  log_warn "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
  
  for i in {30..1}; do
    echo -ne "\rRebooting in $i seconds... "
    sleep 1
  done
  echo ""
}
```

### 5. Progress Estimation

Show time estimates:
```bash
ubuntu_show_progress() {
  local current_hop="$1"
  local total_hops="$2"
  local minutes_per_hop=45
  
  local remaining_hops=$((total_hops - current_hop + 1))
  local remaining_minutes=$((remaining_hops * minutes_per_hop))
  
  log_info "Progress: Upgrade $current_hop of $total_hops"
  log_info "Estimated time remaining: ~${remaining_minutes} minutes"
}
```

## Integration Points

1. Set MOTD at start of upgrade phase
2. Clear MOTD when upgrade completes
3. Lock file acquired before upgrade, released after
4. Status script created during setup, removed during cleanup
5. Pre-reboot warning shown before each reboot","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:18.337553Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:40:19.263020Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:40:19.263020Z","close_reason":"Added lock file functions (upgrade_acquire_lock, upgrade_release_lock), progress display (upgrade_show_progress), reboot countdown warning (upgrade_warn_reboot), and status check script (upgrade_create_status_script). MOTD already implemented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-2yd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:26:42.526199Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:26:42.324328Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lhgm","title":"Create DCG onboarding lesson markdown","description":"## Task: Create DCG onboarding lesson markdown

### What to Do

Create a DCG lesson file for the terminal-based onboarding TUI.

### Location

Create new file: /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs/onboard/lessons/09_dcg.md

### Content Structure

```markdown
# DCG: Pre-Execution Safety Guard

## What is DCG?

DCG (Destructive Command Guard) is a Claude Code hook that blocks dangerous commands BEFORE they execute.

Think of it as a safety net that catches destructive operations and gives you a chance to reconsider.

## What Gets Blocked?

DCG protects against commands like:

**Git Operations:**
- Hard resets that destroy uncommitted work
- Force pushes that overwrite remote history
- Branch force-deletes without merge checks

**Filesystem:**
- Recursive deletes outside temp directories

**Databases:**
- DROP TABLE/DATABASE statements
- TRUNCATE operations

**And more:** Kubernetes deletions, cloud resource termination, etc.

## Testing Commands

Before running something risky, test it first:

    dcg test 'your command here'

Add --explain for details:

    dcg test 'your command here' --explain

## Handling Blocked Commands

When DCG blocks a command, you'll see:
1. Why it was blocked
2. Safer alternatives
3. A short code (like ABC-123)

If you REALLY need to run it:

    dcg allow-once ABC-123

Then run your command within 24 hours.

## Quick Reference

- dcg test 'cmd' - Test if command is safe
- dcg packs --enabled - See active protection
- dcg allow-once CODE - Bypass for legitimate use
- dcg doctor - Check installation status

## Next Steps

DCG works automatically in the background. You don't need to do anything special - just know it's there protecting you!
```

### Rationale

- Keep it concise for terminal reading
- Focus on practical knowledge (testing, allow-once)
- No code examples that might trigger safety hooks
- End with reassurance that it "just works"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:51:06.440962086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:43:40.931038382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:43:40.931038382Z","close_reason":"Already completed - 10_dcg.md exists in acfs/onboard/lessons/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lhgm","depends_on_id":"bd-buxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:53:13.058298082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-lhgm","author":"ubuntu","text":"FIX NEEDED: Change filename from 09_dcg.md to 10_dcg.md since 09_ru.md already exists in acfs/onboard/lessons/","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:36:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ligg","title":"TASK: Add Confetti Celebration on Lesson Completion","description":"Add a simple, delightful celebration when users complete lessons and when they complete ALL lessons.

**Scope (intentionally small):**
1. Confetti burst when clicking 'Mark Complete'
2. Special celebration modal when completing final lesson
3. Encouraging message variations ('Great job!', 'You're crushing it!', etc.)

**Implementation:**
- Use canvas-confetti library (lightweight, no deps)
- Trigger on successful lesson completion
- 2-3 second animation, non-blocking
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion

**What NOT to include (deferred):**
- Streak tracking
- Achievement badges
- Leaderboards
- Certificates
- Time tracking

**Files:**
- apps/web/components/learn/confetti-celebration.tsx
- Integration into lesson-content.tsx

**Why this scope:**
With only 6 lessons, elaborate gamification is overkill. A simple confetti burst provides delight without engineering overhead.  {"id":"bd-llba","title":"Vercel prod JS errors: appendChild invalid token / track 503","description":"Prod user reports catastrophic client crash: `Uncaught SyntaxError: Failed to execute 'appendChild' on 'Node': Invalid or unexpected token` (likely from Next.js <Script> inline analytics injection).

Separately, running Playwright prod smoke tests (`PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=https://agent-flywheel.com`) shows console errors on `/wizard/os-selection`: `Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 503`, consistent with `/api/track` returning 503 when GA env vars fail validation.

Plan:
- Sanitize/validate `NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID` (trim/strip quotes, case-insensitive) across client analytics + server route.
- Ensure inline GA config script embeds measurement id via JSON.stringify (no raw interpolation).
- Ensure client-side Measurement Protocol calls are skipped when GA is not configured.
- Verify: `bun run lint`, `bun run type-check`, `bun run build`, and rerun `bun run test production.spec.ts --project=chromium`.

+json_file_has_usable_jq_value() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"

+

+    [[ -n "$jq_expr" ]] || return 1
+    command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
+
+    while IFS= read -r candidate; do
+        if has_usable_secret "$candidate"; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done < <(jq -r "$jq_expr" "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+json_file_has_usable_string_key() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"
+    shift || true
+
+    [[ -s "$file_path" ]] || return 1
+
+    local key=""
+    local regex=""
+    local line=""
+    for key in "$@"; do
+        [[ -n "$key" ]] || continue
+        regex="\"${key}\"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\"([^\"]+)\""
+        while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+            if [[ "$line" =~ $regex ]] && has_usable_secret "${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"; then
+                return 0
+            fi
+        done < "$file_path"
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+file_has_usable_secret() {
     local file=$1
+    local value=""
+
     [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 1
-    grep -q '[^[:space:]]' "$file" 2>/dev/null
+    value="$(cat "$file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    has_usable_secret "$value"
+}
+
+strip_shell_inline_comment() {
+    local value="${1-}"
+    local quote=""
+    local char=""
+    local prev=""
+    local i
+
+    for (( i = 0; i < ${#value}; i++ )); do
+        char="${value:i:1}"
+
+        if [[ -n "$quote" ]]; then
+            if [[ "$char" == "\\" ]]; then
+                (( i += 1 ))
+                continue
+            fi
+            if [[ "$char" == "$quote" ]]; then
+                quote=""
+            fi
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        if [[ "$char" == '"' || "$char" == "'" ]]; then
+            quote="$char"
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        if [[ "$char" == "#" ]]; then
+            if (( i == 0 )); then
+                printf '%s\n' "${value:0:i}"
+                return 0
+            fi
+            prev="${value:i-1:1}"
+            case "$prev" in
+                [[:space:]])
+                    printf '%s\n' "${value:0:i}"
+                    return 0
+                    ;;
+            esac
+        fi
+    done
+
+    printf '%s\n' "$value"
 }

 }
 
+strip_shell_inline_comment() {
+    local value="${1-}"
+    local quote=""
+    local char=""
+    local prev=""
+    local i
+
+    for (( i = 0; i < ${#value}; i++ )); do
+        char="${value:i:1}"
+
+        if [[ -n "$quote" ]]; then
+            if [[ "$char" == "\\" ]]; then
+                (( i += 1 ))
+                continue
+            fi
+            if [[ "$char" == "$quote" ]]; then
+                quote=""
+            fi
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        if [[ "$char" == '"' || "$char" == "'" ]]; then
+            quote="$char"
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        if [[ "$char" == "#" ]]; then
+            if (( i == 0 )); then
+                printf '%s\n' "${value:0:i}"
+                return 0
+            fi
+            prev="${value:i-1:1}"
+            case "$prev" in
+                [[:space:]])
+                    printf '%s\n' "${value:0:i}"
+                    return 0
+                    ;;
+            esac
+        fi
+    done
+
+    printf '%s\n' "$value"
+}
+

     local file_path="$2"

             local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
-            local first_char="${value:0:1}"
-            if [[ "$first_char" != '"' && "$first_char" != "'" ]]; then
-                value="${value%%#*}"
-            fi
+            value="$(strip_shell_inline_comment "$value")"
             value="$(normalize_config_value "$value")"
             if [[ -n "${value//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
                 printf '%s\n' "$value"

+test_onboard_auth_checks_reject_placeholder_credentials() {
+    setup_installed_layout_env
+
+    mkdir -p "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.claude" "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.codex" "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.gemini" \
+        "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.config/vercel" "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.supabase"
+
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.claude/.credentials.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "claudeAiOauth": {
+    "accessToken": "your-token-here"
+  }
+}
+JSON
+
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.codex/auth.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "tokens": {
+    "access_token": "your_token_here"
+  },
+  "OPENAI_API_KEY": "your_openai_api_key"
+}
+JSON
+
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.gemini/google_accounts.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "active": "replace-me"
+}
+JSON
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "refresh_token": "your-token-here"
+}
+JSON
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.gemini/.env" <<'EOF2'
+GEMINI_API_KEY="YOUR_GEMINI_API_KEY" # replace me
+EOF2
+
+    cat > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.config/vercel/auth.json" <<'JSON'
+{
+  "user": {
+    "email": "tester@example.com"
+  },
+  "token": "your_vercel_token"
+}
+JSON
+
+    printf '%s\n' 'your_supabase_access_token' > "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.supabase/access-token"
+
+    write_fake_command "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/claude" "claude 1.2.3"
+    write_fake_command "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/codex" "codex 1.2.3"
+    write_fake_command "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/gemini" "gemini 1.2.3"
+    write_fake_command "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/vercel" "not logged in"
+    write_fake_command "$TEST_TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/supabase" "supabase 2.99.0"
+
+    local output=""
+    output=$(HOME="$TEST_ROOT_HOME" ACFS_HOME="$TEST_INSTALLED_ACFS" PATH="$TEST_FAKE_BIN:/usr/bin:/bin" bash -lc 'source "'"$ONBOARD_SH"'" help >/dev/null; for svc in claude codex gemini vercel supabase; do check_auth_status "$svc" && rc=0 || rc=$?; printf "%s\n" "$svc=$rc"; done')
+
+    if [[ "$output" == *$'claude=1\n'* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *$'codex=1\n'* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *$'gemini=1\n'* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *$'vercel=1\n'* ]] \
+        && [[ "$output" == *"supabase=1"* ]]; then
+        harness_pass "onboard auth checks reject placeholder credentials"
+    else
+        harness_fail "onboard auth checks reject placeholder credentials" "$output"
+    fi
+
+    cleanup_mock_env
+}
+

     setup_installed_layout_env
